title: Senior Test Automation Engineer
snippet: The Company Cubic³ provides advanced software-defined vehicle solutions to over 200 countries around the world. Our powerfully smart connectivity enables leading automotive, agriculture, and transportation OEMs to deliver innovative new services and fully compliant in-vehicle experiences that customers desire, regardless of local market requirements. We believe in leadership that supports empowerment and responsibility, while recognising and developing leadership qualities across Our Team. Together we bring out the best in each other. So, whether you’re interested in joining us as an individual contributor, manager, senior leader – or someone who aspires to growing into a leadership role – we look for people who are results focused, empathetic, visionary, empowering, and who ‘champion’ our cultures and values. Description Cubic³ is looking for an energetic, enthusiastic Senior Software Test Automation Engineer, who is committed to their work, and capable of working in a fast paced, dynamic environment to join the Test System Team withing the DevOps and Quality Assurance group. This team is responsible for providing test automation solutions, to support the Cubic³ engineering teams by building and maintaining test automation frameworks and tooling. The successful candidate is expected to bring an open, collaborative and innovative approach to their work. Responsibilities • Driving best-in-class test automation principles within the engineering group working as a Senior Subject Matter expert. • Working closely with Engineering Managers and architecture to align and implement best practices. • Expanding and maintaining test automation frameworks for Cubic³ • Implement AI in our automation strategies • Supporting test automation engineers in: · Creating and maintaining automated test suites including mocking frameworks and integrating with and contributing to proprietary testing platforms developed in-house. · Conducting thorough investigation of automation test failures and troubleshooting the outcomes. • Coordinating implementation of necessary tools / environments for test automation engineers, within the established Technology Radar maintained by the Architecture Team. • Integrating with and expanding on Engineering Quality Dashboards to provide standardised test automation visibility and insights across the engineering group. • Help develop and implement training plan to skill-up other engineers in Automation. • Mentor and support other Test Automation Engineers Requirements • Bachelor’s degree in computer science, IT, computer/electronic engineering, or related science discipline. • Minimum 7 years’ experience in a test automation role. • A self-starter with a proven ability to work independently, managing their own time and workload effectively. • Able to understand and communicate thoughts, ideas and design specifications in an unambiguous manner. Is able to effectively communicate with leads and members of cross functional teams. • Comfortable presenting in front of small groups of individual contributors and leadership. • API/Microservice test automation experience. • Web application test automation experience. • Experience working with test automation tools such as /RestSharp/RestAssured/Soap UI/Selenium Webdriver/Load Runner/Jmeter. • Experience working with programming/scripting languages including .Net/C#/C++/python • Experience working in a continuous testing environment and using Continuous Integration tools. • Experience with functional and non-functional test automation. • Strong understanding and knowledge of all phases of testing and standard test automation techniques. • Strong communications and problem-solving skills. • Strong attention to detail Desirables • Ability to act as a mentoring resource for colleagues with less experience. • Experience using AI with Test Automation • Experience using Test Management software. (E.g. TestRail) • Experience using Atlassian tools, e.g., Confluence, Jira • Telecoms/IP Networking experience. • Experience testing IoT/M2M applications or systems. • Experience working with API and web application mocking tools. • Experience working with Cloud infrastructure (Azure, AWS etc). • Experience configuring TLS/SSL on server infrastructure. • Experience working in a lean engineering model, using Kanban. • Mobile Device testing experience. • Working in an Agile development environment. • ISTQB Certification. • Angular Frameworks Testing • Performance Engineering Experience • Experience with UI Automation frameworks • Strong industry experience with leading companies. New hires at Cubic³ are required to work onsite five days a week during their six-month probation period to get to know the company, their team, and our ways of working. After probation, we offer a flexible arrangement of up to eight work-from-home days per month, provided it aligns with business needs and performance standards. This arrangement is not a given for all roles. Cubic³ is an equal opportunities employer and committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace.

title: Core Network Engineer II
snippet: The Company Cubic³ provides advanced software-defined vehicle solutions to over 200 countries around the world. Our powerfully smart connectivity enables leading automotive, agriculture, and transportation OEMs to deliver innovative new services and fully compliant in-vehicle experiences that customers desire, regardless of local market requirements. We believe in leadership that supports empowerment and responsibility, while recognising and developing leadership qualities across Our Team. Together we bring out the best in each other. So, whether you’re interested in joining us as an individual contributor, manager, senior leader – or someone who aspires to growing into a leadership role – we look for people who are results focused, empathetic, visionary, empowering, and who ‘champion’ our cultures and values. The Role We are looking for an energetic, enthusiastic experienced Engineer who has direct working knowledge of Core Networks to include Circuit Switching, Packet Switching, VOLTE, HLR/HSS and messaging (SMS) and is committed to their work and should be comfortable working in a fast-paced, highly flexible environment. The role will be responsible for the support and expansion of a global core network. This is a great opportunity for an engineer with strong Telecoms experience to demonstrate their technical and organisational ability, working in a small team of experienced engineers at the cutting edge of IoT technologies. In this role you will be: Responsible for supporting the Cubic³ core network elements. Performing troubleshooting and analysis of network incidents. Providing input and collaborating with other team members developing, implementing, integrating, and monitoring network team projects. Expanding the network monitoring capability. Ensuring consistent uptime of the core network. Acting as a key point of contact for vendors and other Cubic³ teams. Participating in knowledge transfer to and from fellow engineers. Requirements: Experience with 3GPP Network Elements: HLR/HSS, DRA, STP, SMSC, GMSC, IMS Core, PGW, OCS, PCRF, AAA Good understanding of telecom protocols and corresponding interfaces: GTP (S8), Diameter (S6a, Gx, Gy, Rx, Cx, Sh), MAP, Radius, SMPP, SIP Experience of implementing telecoms integrations. Experience with Wireshark network analyser and scripting languages an advantage. Minimum 5 years’ experience in telecoms Engineering or Degree in Computer/Telecoms Engineering. Proven track record in working with small teams. Demonstrated expertise in troubleshooting network issues. Excellent documentation skills. Desire to learn from and collaborate with peers. Excellent spoken and written English. Previous experience working to aggressive timelines essential. Commitment to provide a high quality and professional service. Ability to work unsupervised and take responsibility for the completion of tasks. Ability to prioritise work tasks. Adaptable and flexible in approach to work. Effective problem-solving skills. Availability to support out-of-hours work where required and on-call rota. New hires at Cubic³ are required to work onsite five days a week during their six-month probation period to get to know the company, their team, and our ways of working. After probation, we offer a flexible arrangement of up to eight work-from-home days per month, provided it aligns with business needs and performance standards. This arrangement is not a given for all roles. Cubic³ is an equal opportunities employer and committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace.

title: Application Support Engineer
snippet: The Company Cubic³ provides advanced software-defined vehicle solutions to over 200 countries around the world. Our powerfully smart connectivity enables leading automotive, agriculture, and transportation OEMs to deliver innovative new services and fully compliant in-vehicle experiences that customers desire, regardless of local market requirements. We believe in leadership that supports empowerment and responsibility, while recognising and developing leadership qualities across Our Team. Together we bring out the best in each other. So, whether you’re interested in joining us as an individual contributor, manager, senior leader – or someone who aspires to growing into a leadership role – we look for people who are results focused, empathetic, visionary, empowering, and who ‘champion’ our cultures and values. The Role We are looking for an energetic, enthusiastic Application Support Engineer who is committed to their work, and should be comfortable working in a fast paced, highly flexible environment. The role will be responsible for providing excellent technical support and customer service as part of our Level 2 support. Responsibilities of this role: Coordinate with support and development teams to resolve application and system issues Design and maintain expertise in flow of application process and systems related to company Administer and resolve applications issues, provide updates and perform root cause analysis Supervise all alerts related to application and system procedures and provide services pro-actively Maintain good professional relationships with counterparts of engineering and customer support departments Organize and implement projects ranging from small to medium size and provide assistance to all processes under guidance of Software Engineers Perform root cause assessment and debug all issues on server domain, and availability of applications Coordinate with test department to assist in releases and deployments to various environments and production outputs Provide support and identify all issues and prepare appropriate documentation all issues and solutions. Manage all production system and recommend ways to optimize performance and provide solution to problems and prepare reports for all problems. Identify and provide resolutions ranging in complexity from medium to high. Essential Experience and Qualifications Knowledge of Microsoft IIS Knowledge of Database technologies Microsoft SQL Server Working Knowledge of IT Hardware and Software Ability to learn and master employer-specific software Complex problem solving Written and verbal communication skills Customer Focused with excellent written and verbal communication and reporting skills Demonstrable ability to learn technical information, processes and applications quickly Excellent attention to detail and the ability to multitask and work effectively within a fast-growing organization Demonstrated ability to be flexible and take a proactive approach to problem solving Desired Experience and Qualifications Experience with cloud infrastructure (Azure) Experience using tools like Postman to create and save simple and complex HTTP/s requests. A degree level qualification in a technical discipline such as Computer Science or Software Engineering Basic knowledge of programming concepts, preferable C# Experience in building effective relationships with development teams and clients New hires at Cubic³ are required to work onsite five days a week during their six-month probation period to get to know the company, their team, and our ways of working. After probation, we offer a flexible arrangement of up to eight work-from-home days per month, provided it aligns with business needs and performance standards. This arrangement is not a given for all roles. Cubic³ is an equal opportunities employer and committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace.

title: Senior Security Engineer
snippet: The Company Cubic³ provides advanced software-defined vehicle solutions to over 200 countries around the world. Our powerfully smart connectivity enables leading automotive, agriculture, and transportation OEMs to deliver innovative new services and fully compliant in-vehicle experiences that customers desire, regardless of local market requirements. We believe in leadership that supports empowerment and responsibility, while recognising and developing leadership qualities across Our Team. Together we bring out the best in each other. So, whether you’re interested in joining us as an individual contributor, manager, senior leader – or someone who aspires to growing into a leadership role – we look for people who are results focused, empathetic, visionary, empowering, and who ‘champion’ our cultures and values. Role Overview Responsible for leading the design, implementation, and continuous improvement of security engineering practices across cloud, on-premise, and hybrid environments. This role drives security-by-design principles, DevSecOps integration, Cloud and platform hardening, while providing technical leadership across the organisation. Key Responsibilities Define and implement security-by-design principles across cloud, on-premise, endpoint, and network environments Embed security into CI/CD pipelines (DevSecOps) in collaboration with engineering and DevOps teams Conduct and lead security risk assessments, ensuring mitigation strategies are implemented across new applications, platforms, and third-party tools Own and enhance cloud, platform, and endpoint security posture, including monitoring for configuration drift and vulnerabilities Develop and enforce hardening standards across endpoints (Windows/macOS), servers, and compute environments Support and partner with teams across IAM, GRC, DLP, SOC, and Vulnerability Management to strengthen overall security capability Own and manage security tools related to the role and discipline e.g. SAST Tooling Drive automation, AI usage and tooling improvements to increase efficiency and reduce risk Provide support to the business as it relates to solution design, project and change management security. Provide technical leadership and incident response support during security events Collaborate with IT, DevOps, Software, Networks and Infra teams to embed security practices Stay current on emerging threats, vulnerabilities, and security technologies Required Qualifications Bachelor’s degree in computer science, Cybersecurity, or related field 8+ years of experience in cybersecurity or information security roles. Experience designing and implementing enterprise scale security principles Proven ability to influence and work with cross-functional teams and departments Hands on experience with end user and cloud security (Azure, AWS, Windows and MAC O/S) Strong technical knowledge of software development (CI/CD pipeline), cloud and security by design ways of working. Experience with security frameworks (NIST, ISO/IEC 27001, NIS2 TISAX) Key Skills Required Security architecture & Zero Trust principles Cloud security (Azure & AWS) DevSecOps and CI/CD security integration (SDLC) Security frameworks & compliance (ISO 27001, NIST, NIS2, TISAX) Workflow Automation & AI adoption Stakeholder management & technical leadership Tool Experience Microsoft Azure (E5 Toolset) - Defender for Cloud, Defender for Endpoint, Defender for Identity, Microsoft Purview AWS Security Hub (CSPM), Amazon Guard Duty, AWS Inspector, AWS Identity Analyser, AWS Config SonarCloud (SAST Tooling) Atlassian Cloud (Confluence / Jira) Vanta (GRC) Strong Microsoft Office 365 knowledge Certifications (Desired) ISC2 - CISSP/CCSP/CSSLP AWS Certified Security - Specialty Microsoft Azure Security Engineer Associate (AZ-500) Microsoft Cybersecurity Architect Expert (SC-100) GIAC GSSP/ GCSA New hires at Cubic³ are required to work onsite five days a week during their six-month probation period to get to know the company, their team, and our ways of working. After probation, we offer a flexible arrangement of up to eight work-from-home days per month, provided it aligns with business needs and performance standards. This arrangement is not a given for all roles. Cubic³ is an equal opportunities employer and committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace.

title: Technical Project Manager
snippet: The Company Cubic³ provides advanced software-defined vehicle solutions to over 200 countries around the world. Our powerfully smart connectivity enables leading automotive, agriculture, and transportation OEMs to deliver innovative new services and fully compliant in-vehicle experiences that customers desire, regardless of local market requirements. We believe in leadership that supports empowerment and responsibility, while recognising and developing leadership qualities across Our Team. Together we bring out the best in each other. So, whether you’re interested in joining us as an individual contributor, manager, senior leader – or someone who aspires to growing into a leadership role – we look for people who are results focused, empathetic, visionary, empowering, and who ‘champion’ our cultures and values. The Role To support Cubic's business, the Cubic Technology Department has built a state-of-the-art platform and core network which supports our customers across the globe. Due to large subscriber base growth, with associated requirements for increased capacity for all our services, as well as the introduction of leading-edge technologies, Cubic is seeking an experienced Technical Project Manager to join our team to drive for optimal Technical Delivery. In this role, the Technical Project Manager will focus on driving technical delivery excellence within the Technology department, supporting cross-department process improvement initiatives, participating as a change agent, measuring the impact of change initiatives, and using data to guide decision-making. They will contribute to the use of key performance indicators (KPIs) and other metrics to track progress and demonstrate the value of change initiatives, using strong influencing skills. This is a great opportunity for an experienced technical project manager to demonstrate their technical and organisational abilities, work with the deployment of cutting-edge IoT technologies in the automotive and agriculture connectivity spaces, and gain insight into all aspects of the Cubic business. Responsibilities will include: Aligning with several domains to develop into a key point of contact for those areas Developing an understanding of industry trends and introducing new ideas Managing the interdependencies and flow of work across multiple teams Supporting short & long-term initiatives across the company Participating in driving lean principles and continuous improvement, using measurement and analysis to support decision-making Identifying, mitigating, and communicating risks in projects & programs to senior leaders and customers, and supporting the resolution of delivery issues Working with the Technical Delivery team to improve team delivery practices Supporting cross-functional engagement within Cubic Requirements 3+ years’ technical project management experience, with 5+ years industry experience, preferably with an engineering background Demonstrated experience in Lean and Agile Methodologies (Lean, Kanban, and other Agile frameworks to optimize workflow and minimize waste) Solid understanding of software delivery Proven ability to support incremental change and continuous improvement Experience contributing to setting, measuring, and driving Objectives and Key Results (OKRs) to align project outcomes with organizational goals Ability to grasp complex technical issues and assist in decision-making Strong organizational and communication skills, with a proven ability to collaborate and guide teams Ability to adapt in a fast-paced environment with changing priorities and challenges Experience using Jira or similar tools to track projects and document processes is advantageous Understanding of connectivity and software platforms is a plus Experience in knowledge sharing and working collaboratively with small teams Excellent spoken and written English New hires at Cubic³ are required to work onsite five days a week during their six-month probation period to get to know the company, their team, and our ways of working. After probation, we offer a flexible arrangement of up to eight work-from-home days per month, provided it aligns with business needs and performance standards. This arrangement is not a given for all roles. Cubic³ is an equal opportunities employer and committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace.

title: Strategy & Corporate Development Analyst
snippet: The Company Cubic³ provides advanced software-defined vehicle solutions to over 200 countries around the world. Our powerfully smart connectivity enables leading automotive, agriculture, and transportation OEMs to deliver innovative new services and fully compliant in-vehicle experiences that customers desire, regardless of local market requirements. We believe in leadership that supports empowerment and responsibility, while recognising and developing leadership qualities across Our Team. Together we bring out the best in each other. So, whether you’re interested in joining us as an individual contributor, manager, senior leader – or someone who aspires to growing into a leadership role – we look for people who are results focused, empathetic, visionary, empowering, and who ‘champion’ our cultures and values. The Role We're now hiring a Strategy & Corporate Development Analyst to help drive this next chapter of growth. This is a unique opportunity to work at the intersection of corporate strategy, growth planning, and M&A in a global, innovation-driven business. You'll work closely with senior leaders across departments—Product, Finance, Commercial, and Technology—to support strategic decisions, evaluate market opportunities, and contribute to inorganic growth initiatives like partnerships, investments, and acquisitions. Ideal for someone early in their career, this role offers broad exposure and fast learning in a high-impact environment. Responsibilities Strategy (50%) Understand global market trends, emerging technologies, and competitors in the connectivity management and broader IoT ecosystem Support development and refinement of the company’s strategic roadmap Help prepare executive presentations and strategic materials for leadership and board meetings Assist in the annual strategic planning cycle and ad hoc strategic initiatives Model market sizing, forecast scenarios, and financial/operational impact of key projects Corporate Development (50%) Support sourcing, evaluation, and execution of M&A, investments, and strategic partnerships Conduct target profiling, due diligence, and market mapping Build valuation models (DCF, comparables, accretion/dilution) and ROI analyses Prepare deal-related materials (e.g., investment memos, internal briefings) Coordinate with internal stakeholders and external advisors (e.g., banks, legal, consultants) Requirements 2–4 years of experience in strategy consulting, investment banking, corporate development, or a high-growth tech environment Bachelor's degree in Finance, Economics, Business, Engineering, or related field Strong analytical and financial modelling skills Excellent written and verbal communication skills—especially for senior audiences Highly organized, detail-oriented, and able to manage multiple workstreams Passion for emerging technology, IoT, and global business Collaborative mindset and comfort working cross-functionally in a fast-paced environment Additional, optional requirements Experience in or exposure to the IoT, telecom, or tech industry Experience supporting or working on M&A transactions Benefits 25 days annual leave + 2 company days Health Insurance Pension Contribution ESOP Tax saver scheme Bike to work scheme Sports and social activities Great annual office outings (Summer BBQ, Christmas party, charity runs) Bits & Bobs days (Half day every quarter to get your Bits & Bobs done) Learning and Development Opportunities Cubic³ is an equal opportunities employer and committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace. New hires at Cubic³ are required to work onsite five days a week during their six-month probation period to get to know the company, their team, and our ways of working. After probation, we offer a flexible arrangement of up to eight work-from-home days per month, provided it aligns with business needs and performance standards. This arrangement is not a given for all roles. Cubic³ is an equal opportunities employer and committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace.

title: Revenue Operations & Assurance Manager
snippet: The Company Cubic³ provides advanced software-defined vehicle solutions to over 200 countries around the world. Our powerfully smart connectivity enables leading automotive, agriculture, and transportation OEMs to deliver innovative new services and fully compliant in-vehicle experiences that customers desire, regardless of local market requirements. We believe in leadership that supports empowerment and responsibility, while recognising and developing leadership qualities across Our Team. Together we bring out the best in each other. So, whether you’re interested in joining us as an individual contributor, manager, senior leader – or someone who aspires to growing into a leadership role – we look for people who are results focused, empathetic, visionary, empowering, and who ‘champion’ our cultures and values. The Role As the Revenue Operations & Assurance Manager , you will lead our Order to Cash activities, overseeing €100m+ in annual Group Revenue across a complex, multi-product global connectivity portfolio. Cubic3 provides a managed IoT connectivity platform, global carrier network access, and tailored connected services to leading automotive and mobile asset OEMs worldwide. This role acts a critical control point in these complex operations, balancing commercial growth with financial integrity. It is a unique opportunity to scale and lead a team accountable for optimising Group Revenue with a direct impact on revenue integrity, margin protection, and cash acceleration. We are looking for a driven and curious individual to use their experience to play a central role in enabling scalable, high-margin growth through best-in-class revenue governance. The successful candidate will report to the Head of Finance Operations and will lead a dedicated team of Revenue Operations and Assurance professionals (2 direct reports) with ambitions to continue to build team capabilities and harness the transformative impact of AI. Role Working closely at the intersection of the Finance, Wholesale and Data & AI teams, the candidate’s key duties will include: Revenue Assurance Implement and manage robust Revenue Assurance processes to ensure timely and accurate revenue and cost reporting. Reconcile activity between network elements, device performance, and billing systems to identify and remediate discrepancies. Perform regular reconciliation and analysis of revenue and cost streams, identifying leakage and opportunities for improvement. Understand and aggregate revenue and cost assurance activities across the business to ensure effectiveness and accuracy. Design and implement AI-enabled revenue assurance frameworks to ensure proactive identification and remediation of revenue risks. Leverage predictive analytics and data visualisation platforms (Power BI, Looker, etc.) to proactively identify revenue leakage and generate actionable insights for the business. Drive automation across billing and assurance processes, reducing manual effort and improving accuracy and speed of execution. Provide assurance to the Finance Department for the settlement of roaming payments and receipts. Billing Operations Own end-to-end billing operations, including invoice generation, dispatch, and reconciliation across customer and partner accounts. Maintain billing system (NetSuite) to ensure accuracy for both revenues and costs. Lead the resolution of billing disputes, processing credits and adjustments in a timely and accurate manner. Serve as the primary point of escalation for customer and partner billing queries, ensuring issues are resolved promptly and root causes are addressed. Coordinate with other departments to onboard new revenue product implementation and monthly billing activities. Work closely with the Financial Controller on cash forecasting analysis. Stakeholder Management & Reporting Drive business awareness and resolution of billing and revenue initiatives, as well as ad-hoc analysis as required. Analyse data and reports to identify opportunities and areas for improvement across revenue and billing operations. Act as the key Finance liaison for billing and revenue matters across Commercial, Wholesale, and Data & AI teams. Leadership Lead, scale, and performance-manage a growing team of revenue assurance and billing operations professionals, fostering a high-performance culture aligned to Cubic’s growth ambitions. Develop team skills and capabilities with structured training, defined goals, and ongoing coaching and mentorship. Lead the team through transformation — driving process change, embedding new ways of working, and championing a culture of accuracy, continuous improvement, and accountability as the function evolves. Requirements Minimum of 5 years’ experience in a Revenue Assurance or Billing Operations role, with at least 2 years in a team leadership or management capacity Hold a degree-level qualification in Finance, Business, or a related field Versatile team player – enthusiastic, flexible, pragmatic, thorough, pro-active Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills, with demonstrable experience of driving impact across the business Excellent attention to detail Additional, Desirable Requirements Experience working in a telecommunications, IoT or connectivity domain is highly desirable Qualified accountant (ACA/ACCA/CIMA) with experience in a global finance organisation. Hands-on experience with NetSuite or a comparable billing/ERP platform in an operational finance environment. This is an opportunity to build and lead a best-in-class Revenue Operations and Assurance function in a high-growth, global connectivity business with direct impact on financial performance, commercial scalability, and the future of Cubic’s growth ambitions. New hires at Cubic³ are required to work onsite five days a week during their six-month probation period to get to know the company, their team, and our ways of working. After probation, we offer a flexible arrangement of up to eight work-from-home days per month, provided it aligns with business needs and performance standards. This arrangement is not a given for all roles. Cubic³ is an equal opportunities employer and committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace.

title: Director of Software Engineering
snippet: The Company Cubic³ provides advanced software-defined vehicle solutions to over 200 countries around the world. Our powerfully smart connectivity enables leading automotive, agriculture, and transportation OEMs to deliver innovative new services and fully compliant in-vehicle experiences that customers desire, regardless of local market requirements. We believe in leadership that supports empowerment and responsibility, while recognising and developing leadership qualities across Our Team. Together we bring out the best in each other. So, whether you’re interested in joining us as an individual contributor, manager, senior leader – or someone who aspires to growing into a leadership role – we look for people who are results focused, empathetic, visionary, empowering, and who ‘champion’ our cultures and values. We are seeking a visionary Director of Engineering to lead Cubic³’s full Engineering organisation across Software Development and SIM Systems , driving the technical excellence, scalability, and reliability of all our platforms and products. This role is for a strategic, people‑centric, and technically strong executive who thrives in a fast‑paced environment and can lead complex teams across multiple domains while shaping the future of global connected mobility. The Role The Director of Engineering is accountable for Cubic³’s entire engineering output, maturity, and long‑term technology direction. You will lead a multi‑disciplinary organisation including Software Engineering, Platform/SRE, SIM Technology and QA/Automation ensuring that Cubic³ continues to deliver world‑class, highly available, secure, compliant, and innovative solutions. You will partner closely with Product, Operations, IT, Commercial, and the Executive Leadership Team to define technology strategy, align on business priorities, and drive Cubic³’s roadmap across all customer segments. You will also be a key voice in shaping Cubic³’s platform evolution, SIM innovation, cloud‑native architectures, and the next generation of connected intelligence services. This is a senior leadership role responsible for organisational scale, engineering effectiveness, and the reliability of platforms used by global automotive OEMs and Tier‑1 partners. Responsibilities Executive Leadership & Strategy Define and own Cubic³’s Engineering vision, strategy, and long‑term technology roadmap across software, network, and SIM domains. Lead a high‑performing, multi‑disciplinary engineering organisation with clear structures, succession pipelines, and leadership development. Work with the ELT to set business priorities, investment decisions, and multi‑year strategic plans. Champion engineering excellence, scalability, automation, and performance across all systems. Technical & Architectural Ownership Oversee architectural direction for Cubic³’s core platforms, cloud services, and data systems. Ensure robust engineering governance across security, compliance, quality, and operational readiness. Drive modernisation initiatives such as cloud‑native transformation, network virtualisation, microservices evolution, and advanced automation. Delivery, Operations & Reliability Ensure on‑time, high‑quality delivery of major engineering programmes and customer commitments. Own global platform performance, availability, reliability, and scalability in partnership with SRE and Network teams. Oversee SIM lifecycle management, ensuring world‑class resilience and compliance with telecom standards. Establish KPIs and engineering metrics across cycle time, quality, reliability, and operational health. People Leadership & Culture Foster a culture of innovation, empowerment, accountability, and continuous improvement. Build and grow engineering leadership talent, ensuring strong management capability across all domains. Promote cross‑functional collaboration and transparent communication. Stakeholder & Customer Engagement Act as the executive engineering representative with key customers, partners, and stakeholders. Support commercial teams in pre‑sales and strategic customer engagements. Ensure alignment between Product, Engineering, and Operations for efficient delivery and lifecycle management. Requirements Essential 10+ years in engineering leadership, including at least 5 years leading directors/managers in a multi‑disciplinary technology organisation. Proven experience overseeing software, network, and/or telecom platform engineering at scale. Deep understanding of cloud architectures (AWS/Azure/GCP), distributed systems, and reliability engineering. Strong expertise in telecom/IoT domains such as eSIM, core network technologies, or large‑scale connectivity platforms. Demonstrated success running large engineering organisations (25+ people) with complex delivery portfolios. Exceptional executive communication skills, stakeholder management, and cross‑functional leadership. Experience leading transformation or large‑scale modernisation initiatives. Desirable Experience in automotive, IoT connectivity, or global telecom environments. Knowledge of 4G/5G core network components, SIM/eSIM technologies, and connectivity lifecycle management. Familiarity with high‑availability architectures, Kubernetes, microservices, and CI/CD automation. Background in data analytics, telemetry systems, or connected intelligence platforms. PhD/ M.Sc in Computer Science or Advanced degree an advantage. New hires at Cubic³ are required to work onsite five days a week during their six-month probation period to get to know the company, their team, and our ways of working. After probation, we offer a flexible arrangement of up to eight work-from-home days per month, provided it aligns with business needs and performance standards. This arrangement is not a given for all roles. Cubic³ is an equal opportunities employer and committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace.

title: Roaming Associate
snippet: The Company Cubic³ provides advanced software-defined vehicle solutions to over 200 countries around the world. Our powerfully smart connectivity enables leading automotive, agriculture, and transportation OEMs to deliver innovative new services and fully compliant in-vehicle experiences that customers desire, regardless of local market requirements. We believe in leadership that supports empowerment and responsibility, while recognising and developing leadership qualities across Our Team. Together we bring out the best in each other. Role Overview The successful candidate will support the expansion and optimisation of our global roaming network by managing roaming partnerships, contracts, and commercial performance. This role requires strong coordination skills, a collaborative mindset, and a solid understanding of roaming commercial and operational frameworks. Key Responsibilities Coordinate and support roaming contract management across global MNO partners Manage and grow existing roaming partnerships , while identifying opportunities to establish new roaming relationships to expand network coverage Lead or support contract negotiations with MNO partners, including AA12/13, AA73, and Master Service Agreements Act as a primary interface for relationship management with assigned roaming partners Work closely with the Wholesale team to define roaming network requirements and translate these into actionable initiatives Administer roaming contracts and associated documentation, ensuring accuracy across internal reporting, sales, and operational tools Conduct regular traffic and usage trend analysis to identify optimisation opportunities and drive cost efficiencies Required Experience & Skills 2–3 years’ experience in a corporate environment, preferably within the telecommunications or wholesale domain Strong coordination, follow-up, and organisational skills , with the ability to manage multiple priorities Proven ability to work cross-functionally with commercial, technical, and operational teams Strategic mindset with a “can-do” attitude , comfortable working on complex and evolving challenges Genuine interest in relationship-building and participation in commercial and contractual discussions Basic understanding of roaming commercial and/or technical setups Positive, constructive team player with a willingness to go beyond the call of duty to ensure timely and high-quality delivery Able to analyse data and processes, identify trends, and make well-founded commercial or strategic recommendations New hires at Cubic³ are required to work onsite five days a week during their six-month probation period to get to know the company, their team, and our ways of working. title: SVP of Product
snippet: The Company Cubic³ provides advanced software-defined vehicle solutions to over 200 countries around the world. Our powerfully smart connectivity enables leading automotive, agriculture, and transportation OEMs to deliver innovative new services and fully compliant in-vehicle experiences that customers desire, regardless of local market requirements. We believe in leadership that supports empowerment and responsibility, while recognising and developing leadership qualities across Our Team. Together we bring out the best in each other. So, whether you’re interested in joining us as an individual contributor, manager, senior leader – or someone who aspires to growing into a leadership role – we look for people who are results focused, empathetic, visionary, empowering, and who ‘champion’ our cultures and values. Role Overview Reporting to the Chief Corporate Officer (CCO) , the SVP of Product is the primary driver of Cubic’s product portfolio strategy and market-led innovation. This role is responsible for transforming the company’s technology capabilities into commercially successful, customer-centric products that position Cubic as a leader in global connectivity solutions. The SVP of Product will define the "what" and the "why" of our roadmap, ensuring our platforms scale from 25M to 100M+ assets by delivering autonomous, intelligent, and secure services that meet the specific needs of automotive, agricultural, and transportation customers worldwide. Strategic Mandate Market Vision: Define a bold product vision that anticipates industry shifts in IoT, autonomous driving, and AI-driven services. Commercial Alignment: Partner with the CCO to ensure the product roadmap aligns with corporate growth objectives, revenue targets, and market expansion. Portfolio Strategy: Translate complex industry shifts (autonomous, V2X, embedded AI) into a coherent, prioritized strategic roadmap. Customer Value: Embed AI/ML and advanced analytics into products to unlock new revenue streams and deliver superior customer experiences. Product Evangelism: Represent Cubic as a product thought leader in industry forums, shaping how OEMs perceive the future of connected mobility. Key Responsibilities Roadmap Ownership: Own the end-to-end product roadmap for connectivity platforms and software-defined vehicle services. Lifecycle Management: Oversee the full product lifecycle from initial concept and proof-of-concept in innovation labs to global commercialization. Cross-Functional Collaboration: Work closely with the CTO and technical teams to ensure architectural innovations in cloud and edge computing meet market requirements. Reporting & Governance: Provide regular strategic updates to the CCO and executive team regarding investment priorities and product performance. M&A Support: Conduct product due diligence for potential partnerships and M&A activities to ensure alignment with the existing ecosystem. Financial Stewardship: Manage product-related budgets and optimize pricing and cost structures for global scale. Team Leadership: Build and mentor a high-performing product management organization characterized by curiosity and strategic clarity. Skills & Competencies Strategic Intuition: Ability to anticipate future market trends and translate them into actionable product strategies. Domain Expertise: Deep knowledge of IoT ecosystems, SaaS platforms, and how AI/ML can be productized for the automotive sector. Commercial Acumen: Exceptional ability to link product decisions to business outcomes, ROI, and customer value. Stakeholder Management: Exceptional ability to inspire internal teams and engage global OEM stakeholders. Experience Profile Senior Leadership: 15+ years in product or technology leadership, with 5+ years at the SVP or C-suite level. Scale: Proven success in managing products that have scaled from tens of millions to hundreds of millions of users or assets. Industry Background: Deep experience in connected services, automotive ecosystems, and IoT. Product-Tech Integration: Demonstrated ability to lead integrated product functions in high-growth, global environments, working in tandem with engineering and corporate strategy. New hires at Cubic³ are required to work onsite five days a week during their six-month probation period to get to know the company, their team, and our ways of working. After probation, we offer a flexible arrangement of up to eight work-from-home days per month, provided it aligns with business needs and performance standards. This arrangement is not a given for all roles. Cubic³ is an equal opportunities employer and committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace.

title: Network Operations Engineer
snippet: The Company Cubic³ provides advanced software-defined vehicle solutions to over 200 countries around the world. Our powerfully smart connectivity enables leading automotive, agriculture, and transportation OEMs to deliver innovative new services and fully compliant in-vehicle experiences that customers desire, regardless of local market requirements. We believe in leadership that supports empowerment and responsibility, while recognising and developing leadership qualities across Our Team. Together we bring out the best in each other. So, whether you’re interested in joining us as an individual contributor, manager, senior leader – or someone who aspires to growing into a leadership role – we look for people who are results focused, empathetic, visionary, empowering, and who ‘champion’ our cultures and values. The Company Cubic³ provides advanced software-defined vehicle solutions to over 200 countries around the world. Our powerfully smart connectivity enables leading automotive, agriculture, and transportation OEMs to deliver innovative new services and fully compliant in-vehicle experiences that customers desire, regardless of local market requirements. We believe in leadership that supports empowerment and responsibility, while recognising and developing leadership qualities across Our Team. Together we bring out the best in each other. So, whether you’re interested in joining us as an individual contributor, manager, senior leader – or someone who aspires to growing into a leadership role – we look for people who are results focused, empathetic, visionary, empowering, and who ‘champion’ our cultures and values The Role We are looking for an energetic, enthusiastic Network Operations Engineer who is committed to their work and thrives in a fast-paced, highly flexible environment. In this role, you will be a key part of the team ensuring the seamless operation of Cubic³'s global connectivity platform, supporting critical services for IoT devices, automotive solutions, and mobile networks. You will take ownership of troubleshooting and resolving complex network issues, particularly in relation to SS7, Diameter, S6a signalling, VoLTE, SMS, and CS voice. Your day-to-day will involve proactive monitoring, deep technical analysis, and the use of advanced diagnostic tools to identify and address problems related to network attach failures, data session setup issues, and performance inconsistencies during roaming scenarios. Collaboration is key in this role. You’ll work cross-functionally with internal engineering teams, external vendors, and MNO partners to resolve issues efficiently and ensure minimal disruption to services. You’ll also contribute to continuous improvement initiatives by sharing knowledge, mentoring colleagues, and refining operational processes to enhance service quality and network stability. You will be encouraged to develop innovative solutions, participate in cross-team problem-solving, and actively engage in efforts to optimise network performance. Responsibilities of this role: Support the introduction of new services, ensuring they meet technical and performance standards. Work closely with Cubic vendors to ensure timely delivery and optimal performance of services. Proactively monitor and troubleshoot network issues, particularly with 3G/LTE attach failures, data session setup in roaming scenarios, and app-specific data throughput inconsistencies, ensuring minimal disruption to Cubic customers. Collaborate with internal stakeholders and customers to promptly resolve service-affecting issues. Conduct deep technical investigations using protocol analysis tools (e.g., Wireshark) to identify and resolve signalling and data session-related problems. Work with MNO partners to facilitate the seamless introduction of new services, including managing configurations and troubleshooting signalling issues during service deployment. Support team members by sharing knowledge and expertise, mentoring less experienced colleagues on signalling protocols and troubleshooting methodologies. Where necessary, schedule and execute network maintenance activities, including upgrades and migrations, to ensure ongoing network health. Lead incident resolution efforts, especially for escalated cases involving complex signalling issues or roaming-related problems, performing root cause analysis and implementing long-term fixes. Continuously improve operational processes by reviewing and refining troubleshooting workflows and documenting lessons learned from incidents, enhancing both team efficiency and service reliability. Support the enforcement of change, release, incident, and problem management processes to ensure consistency, minimise service disruptions, and maintain network stability. Requirements Minimum of 4 years’ experience working in the core network domain. Strong knowledge of SS7 protocol to diagnose and resolve signalling issues. Familiarity with Diameter signalling, particularly the S6a interface for handling authentication, authorisation, and mobility management. Experience diagnosing VoLTE-related call drops, quality issues, and troubleshooting SIP signalling in IMS networks and an ability to troubleshoot issues in traditional CS voice networks, including handover failures, call drops, and quality concerns. Deep understanding of SMS signalling, including delivery issues, message routing, and troubleshooting MT/MO message failures. Knowledge of mobile roaming solutions and related technologies, including, a solid understanding of 3G and LTE attach procedures, especially in roaming scenarios. Experience in resolving location update failures and authentication problems. Expertise in diagnosing PDP context setup issues, identifying problems with APN configuration, DNS resolution failures, and identifying conflicts in P-GW/GGSN configurations during roaming. Proven ability to troubleshoot inconsistent data throughput for specific apps during roaming scenarios, including firewall issues, MTU mismatches, or misconfigured application-layer protocols. Ability to quickly comprehend internal Core network call-flows or map out undocumented call-flows step by step, tracing each hop to ensure a clear understanding Ability to use tools like Wireshark, tcpdump, or similar to analyse signalling traffic and diagnose protocol-specific issues across SS7, Diameter, GTP, and SIP layers. Experience working with external vendors to escalate complex issues, provide logs, traces, and collaborate on fixes for signalling or session-related problems. Familiarity with network monitoring and performance management tools (e.g., Zabbix, PRTG, SolarWinds) to detect and troubleshoot network issues proactively. Strong documentation skills for incident reports and post-incident root cause analysis, with an ability to break down complex problems into digestible reports. Desired Experience and Qualifications Understanding of underlying IP network architecture and the ability to perform initial issue analysis. Experience with ITIL processes is an advantage. Familiarity with multi-IMSI solutions, and eUICC SIM profile downloads New hires at Cubic³ are required to work onsite five days a week during their six-month probation period to get to know the company, their team, and our ways of working. After probation, we offer a flexible arrangement of up to eight work-from-home days per month, provided it aligns with business needs and performance standards. This arrangement is not a given for all roles. Cubic³ is an equal opportunities employer and committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace.

title: Financial Accountant
snippet: The Company Cubic³ provides advanced software-defined vehicle solutions to over 200 countries around the world. Our powerfully smart connectivity enables leading automotive, agriculture, and transportation OEMs to deliver innovative new services and fully compliant in-vehicle experiences that customers desire, regardless of local market requirements. We believe in leadership that supports empowerment and responsibility, while recognising and developing leadership qualities across Our Team. Together we bring out the best in each other. So, whether you’re interested in joining us as an individual contributor, manager, senior leader – or someone who aspires to growing into a leadership role – we look for people who are results focused, empathetic, visionary, empowering, and who ‘champion’ our cultures and values. The Role As our new Financial Accountant , you will be at the heart of Cubic’s Finance Operations function – delivering accurate financial reporting, strengthening controls, and providing the insights that enable confident decision-making across the Group. As a company, Cubic3 provides a managed IoT connectivity platform, global carrier network access, and tailored connected services to leading automotive and mobile asset OEMs worldwide. This role sits within a finance team that is responsible for the timely, accurate and compliant reporting of these operations. The team is accountable for the consolidated Group results of 20+ subsidiaries across the world, external and internal audit, local subsidiary management of accounting and tax providers, and periodic reporting to our investors, including one of Japan’s largest PLCs. We’re looking for a qualified accountant who combines strong technical foundations with genuine curiosity and a passion for challenging the status quo. As a valued member of the Finance team, you’ll work cross-functionally with Legal, Commercial, FP&A and other functional teams – building the kind of broad commercial awareness that accelerates long-term career growth. This is a hands-on role with real scope to influence how the finance function evolves as Cubic scales. This role promises the skills and opportunity for future career development to an ambitious accountant. The candidate’s key duties will include: Financial reporting and month-end close • Lead an assigned set of month-end close activities to ensure accurate and timely financial reporting, including preparation of journal entries, accruals, and variance analysis. • Take full ownership of a set of Cubic subsidiary entities including monthly management reporting, annual financial reporting and financial statement preparation. This will include owning the relationship with our local providers for related matters, ensuring that all requirements are met on time to the highest standards. • Prepare management reports that go beyond the numbers – delivering deep analysis of key metrics, trends, and variances that influence business direction. Balance sheet and reconciliations • Manage and review complex balance sheet reconciliations, maintaining financial integrity and strengthening technical accounting skills through real-world application. • Perform regular reconciliation and analysis of financial data, identifying discrepancies and driving timely resolution. Compliance and tax • Help oversee statutory reporting and end-to-end tax compliance (VAT, VIES, MOSS, RTD) across the Cubic entities, gaining hands-on experience with both domestic and cross-border tax obligations. • Support the preparation of statutory financial statements and ensure adherence to relevant accounting standards and regulatory requirements. Audit • Act as a Finance point of contact for external and internal audits (including with SoftBank) for your assigned processes and entities, building insight into risk management and compliance at an international level. • Prepare audit schedules, respond to auditor queries, and drive the timely completion of audit deliverables. Process improvement and automation • As a team, we continually strive to identify automation opportunities across the finance function, reducing manual effort and increasing strategic value – including exploring how AI can our enhance day-to-day processes. • Champion best practices and embed continuous improvement into the team’s ways of working. Business partnering • Collaborate cross-functionally with Legal, Commercial, FP&A and other departmental functions to ensure the finance function supports the wider business effectively. Requirements • Fully qualified accountant (ACA, ACCA, CIMA, or equivalent) with a solid technical foundation and commercial awareness • Post-qualification experience in a dynamic finance environment is preferable, ideally within a fast-paced or international business • Proven hands-on experience with month-end close processes, financial reporting, or audit preparation • Strong financial acumen with the ability to scrutinise, validate, and sense-check complex financial data with confidence • Versatile team player – enthusiastic, flexible, pragmatic, thorough, and proactive Skills & Attributes that will set you apart: • Detail-driven mindset with high standards for accuracy, quality, and integrity • Systems-savvy – experience with Oracle NetSuite is a strong advantage, but a quick learner on ERP platforms is welcome • Adaptable and collaborative – thrives in a team environment while also confident working independently • Naturally curious and proactive – keeps up to date with evolving accounting standards and technology innovations • Strong communicator – comfortable translating numbers into insights for finance and non-finance stakeholders alike • Self-starter with initiative – enjoys taking ownership and driving continuous improvement This is an opportunity to join a best-in-class Finance Operations function in a high-growth, global connectivity business with direct exposure to international reporting, cross-functional collaboration, and the commercial drivers behind Cubic’s ambitious growth trajectory. New hires at Cubic³ are required to work onsite five days a week during their six-month probation period to get to know the company, their team, and our ways of working. After probation, we offer a flexible arrangement of up to eight work-from-home days per month, provided it aligns with business needs and performance standards. This arrangement is not a given for all roles. Cubic³ is an equal opportunities employer and committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace.

title: Core Network Engineer II
snippet: The Company Cubic³ provides advanced software-defined vehicle solutions to over 200 countries around the world. Our powerfully smart connectivity enables leading automotive, agriculture, and transportation OEMs to deliver innovative new services and fully compliant in-vehicle experiences that customers desire, regardless of local market requirements. We believe in leadership that supports empowerment and responsibility, while recognising and developing leadership qualities across Our Team. Together we bring out the best in each other. So, whether you’re interested in joining us as an individual contributor, manager, senior leader – or someone who aspires to growing into a leadership role – we look for people who are results focused, empathetic, visionary, empowering, and who ‘champion’ our cultures and values. The Role We are looking for an energetic, enthusiastic experienced Engineer who has direct working knowledge of Core Networks to include Circuit Switching, Packet Switching, VOLTE, HLR/HSS and messaging (SMS) and is committed to their work and should be comfortable working in a fast-paced, highly flexible environment. The role will be responsible for the support and expansion of a global core network. This is a great opportunity for an engineer with strong Telecoms experience to demonstrate their technical and organisational ability, working in a small team of experienced engineers at the cutting edge of IoT technologies. In this role you will be: Responsible for supporting the Cubic³ core network elements. Performing troubleshooting and analysis of network incidents. Providing input and collaborating with other team members developing, implementing, integrating, and monitoring network team projects. Expanding the network monitoring capability. Ensuring consistent uptime of the core network. Acting as a key point of contact for vendors and other Cubic³ teams. Participating in knowledge transfer to and from fellow engineers. Requirements: Experience with 3GPP Network Elements: HLR/HSS, DRA, STP, SMSC, GMSC, IMS Core, PGW, OCS, PCRF, AAA Good understanding of telecom protocols and corresponding interfaces: GTP (S8), Diameter (S6a, Gx, Gy, Rx, Cx, Sh), MAP, Radius, SMPP, SIP Experience of implementing telecoms integrations. Experience with Wireshark network analyser and scripting languages an advantage. Minimum 5 years’ experience in telecoms Engineering or Degree in Computer/Telecoms Engineering. Proven track record in working with small teams. Demonstrated expertise in troubleshooting network issues. Excellent documentation skills. Desire to learn from and collaborate with peers. Excellent spoken and written English. Previous experience working to aggressive timelines essential. Commitment to provide a high quality and professional service. Ability to work unsupervised and take responsibility for the completion of tasks. Ability to prioritise work tasks. Adaptable and flexible in approach to work. Effective problem-solving skills. Availability to support out-of-hours work where required and on-call rota. New hires at Cubic³ are required to work onsite five days a week during their six-month probation period to get to know the company, their team, and our ways of working. After probation, we offer a flexible arrangement of up to eight work-from-home days per month, provided it aligns with business needs and performance standards. This arrangement is not a given for all roles. Cubic³ is an equal opportunities employer and committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace.

title: Test Automation Engineer
snippet: The Company Cubic³ provides advanced software-defined vehicle solutions to over 200 countries around the world. Our powerfully smart connectivity enables leading automotive, agriculture, and transportation OEMs to deliver innovative new services and fully compliant in-vehicle experiences that customers desire, regardless of local market requirements. We believe in leadership that supports empowerment and responsibility, while recognising and developing leadership qualities across Our Team. Together we bring out the best in each other. The Role We are looking for an energetic and motivated Test Automation Engineer to join our DevOps and Quality Engineering team. This role will focus on building, maintaining, and executing automated test solutions that support our engineering teams. You will work closely with developers, QA engineers, and DevOps teams to help ensure high-quality, scalable software delivery. This is an excellent opportunity for someone with solid automation experience who is looking to deepen their technical expertise and grow towards a senior or specialist role. Key Responsibilities Design, develop, and maintain automated test suites across multiple systems and services Contribute to the evolution of test automation frameworks and tooling Execute automated and manual tests as required to validate features and releases Investigate and troubleshoot test failures , identifying root causes and working with engineering teams on fixes Collaborate with developers to ensure testability and quality are embedded early in the development lifecycle Support integration of automated testing into CI/CD pipelines Contribute to quality metrics and reporting , helping improve visibility of test outcomes Ensure best practices in test automation are followed across the team Participate in code reviews and continuous improvement initiatives Experience & Skills Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related discipline 3–5 years’ experience in software testing or test automation Hands-on experience with test automation tools and frameworks (e.g. Selenium, Cypress, Playwright or similar) Experience working with programming languages including .Net/C#/C++ Performance Testing experience, including Load and Stress testing Understanding of API testing and test automation strategies Experience working within Agile / Scrum environments Exposure to CI/CD tools (e.g. Jenkins, GitHub Actions, GitLab CI) Strong debugging and problem-solving skills Good communication and collaboration skills Nice to Have Experience with cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, or GCP) Knowledge of containerisation (Docker, Kubernetes) Exposure to performance or security testing tools Familiarity with telecoms, IoT, or connected device ecosystems New hires at Cubic³ are required to work onsite five days a week during their six-month probation period to get to know the company, their team, and our ways of working.
